name: CDC Wastewater Data for Measles
description:
This dataset provides a complete time history of measles wastewater sample data and calculated metrics from US sampling locations.

CDC’s National Wastewater Surveillance System (NWSS) includes data collected and reported by:
• All data with a source designation of “state_territory” were generated by state and local health departments are reported to CDC and supported with funding through the Epidemiology and Laboratory Capacity for the Prevention and Control of Emerging Infectious Diseases (ELC) Cooperative Agreement (https://www.cdc.gov/epidemiology-laboratory-capacity/php/our-work/index.html).
• All data with a source designation of “cdc_verily” were generated by CDC’s national wastewater testing contract (currently with Verily Life Sciences, LLC).
• All data with a source designation of “wws” were generated by WastewaterSCAN, a partnership between Stanford University, Emory University, and Verily. All results are understood to be based on inputs that are experimental in nature and are not intended to diagnose or treat any disease. The results are provided “as is” and without warranty of any kind. Stanford does not accept liability for any claim arising out of or in connection with the disclosure of these results. WastewaterSCAN indicates that by accessing or copying any part of the database, the user accepts the terms of Stanford’s license (CC BY-NC 4.0). These data are being made available to inform public health decision making. Anyone seeking to use the database for other purposes or for research is required to contact the WastewaterSCAN / SCAN team (email: wwscan_stanford_emory@lists.stanford.edu) and any use of the data should be cited appropriately (https://data.wastewaterscan.org/about/#18).

Learn more at: https://www.cdc.gov/nwss/index.html.

This dataset is updated weekly on Fridays.

columns.name: record_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for each row of data.

columns.name: sewershed_id
columns.description: A unique identifier for wastewater treatment plants. This is an arbitrary integer used to provide a unique, but anonymous identifier for a wastewater treatment plant. This identifier is consistent over time, such that the same plant retains the same ID regardless of the addition or subtraction of other plants from the data set.

columns.name: wwtp_jurisdiction
columns.description: State, DC, US territory, or Freely Associated State jurisdiction name (2-letter abbreviation) in which the wastewater treatment plant is located.

columns.name: source
columns.description: The source of the data collected and reported to CDC's Wastewater Surveillance Program, including public health departments (state_territory), CDC's national wastewater testing contract (cdc_verily, or formerly, cdc_biobot) and an academic partner group WastewaterSCAN (wws). For additional information see: https://www.cdc.gov/nwss/about.html

columns.name: county_fips
columns.description: 5-digit numeric FIPS codes of all counties and county equivalents served by this sampling site (i.e., served by this wastewater treatment plant or, if 'sample_location' is "upstream", then by this upstream location). Note that most jurisdictions are covered by counties, but some are covered by county equivalents, such as independent cities, parishes, or census areas.

columns.name: counties_served
columns.description: The county and county-equivalent names corresponding to the FIPS codes in 'county_fips'.

columns.name: population_served
columns.description: Estimated number of persons served by this sampling site (i.e., served by this wastewater treatment plant or, if 'sample_location' is "upstream", then by this upstream location)

columns.name: sample_id
columns.description: An anonymized ID assigned to wastewater samples. Wastewater samples for which multiple pathogen PCR targets are measured should have the same sample ID.

columns.name: sample_collect_date
columns.description: Sample collection date

columns.name: sample_type
columns.description: Type of sample collected, whether grab or composite. If composite, the duration of sampling and type of composite is specified (e.g., "24-hr flow-weighted composite"). A grab sample is defined as an individual sample collected without compositing or adding other samples, regardless of whether the sample matrix is liquid wastewater or sludge.

columns.name: sample_matrix
columns.description: Wastewater matrix from which the sample was collected. Wastewater matrix is a categorical variable specifying the type of wastewater sample used (e.g., raw influent, primary sludge, settled solids).

columns.name: sample_location
columns.description: Sample collection location in the wastewater system, whether at a wastewater treatment plant (or other community level treatment infrastructure such as community-scale septic) or upstream in the wastewater system

columns.name: flow_rate
columns.description: Wastewater volumetric flow rate at the sample collection location over the 24-hr period during which the sample was collected. If only an instantaneous flow measurement is available, it may be reported in units of million gallons per day.

columns.name: concentration_method
columns.description: Laboratory method used to concentrate (or enrich) the sample prior to analysis of the concentrate. This procedures concentrates pathogen titers from a larger volume of wastewater prior to extraction and molecular detection.

top.item: membrane filtration with acidification and mgcl2
thermo magmax wastewater ultra nucleic acid isolation kit
membrane filtration with addition of mgcl2
promega wastewater large volume tna capture kit

columns.name: pasteurized
columns.description: A yes/no flag indicating whether  the sample pasteurized or heat treated.

columns.name: pcr_type
columns.description: The type of PCR (e.g., qPCR, dPCR, ddPCR) used to quantify the PCR target

columns.name: extraction_method
columns.description: Laboratory method used to purify the genetic material (RNA or DNA) of the PCR target by extraction from the sample.

cachedContents.largest: thermo magmax wastewater ultra nucleic acid isolation kit

top.item: thermo magmax viral/pathogen nucleic acid isolation kit
monarch total rna miniprep kit (new england biolabs) + onestep pcr inhibitor removal kit (zymo)
thermo magmax microbiome ultra nucleic acid isolation kit
nuclisens manual magnetic bead extraction kit
thermo magmax wastewater ultra nucleic acid isolation kit
applied biosystems magmax wastewater ultra nucleic acid isolation kit

cachedContents.smallest: applied biosystems magmax wastewater ultra nucleic acid isolation kit

columns.name: major_lab_method
columns.description: A number used to distinguish major lab methods at the reporting jurisdiction level. Differences in lab methods may limit the comparability of PCR target concentrations. Reporting jurisdictions should use this field to indicate that lab methods are sufficiently different that caution should be used when comparing PCR target concentrations across them. These differences may result from any lab processing or quantification steps, including different PCR gene targets. Note that 'major_lab_method' may be the same for different laboratories if the reporting jurisdiction believes concentrations measured by those laboratories are comparable; conversely, 'major_lab_method' may be different for the same laboratory if changes made to the laboratory protocol rendered concentrations not comparable. Also note that major lab method is only comparable across reporting site and should not be compared across different sites.

columns.name: inhibition_detect
columns.description: A yes/no flag indicating whether inhibition was detected in the assay. A "yes" value means that amplification or detection are suppressed due to interfering substances in the sample, while a "no" value means inhibition was not detected. Detecting inhibition is essential for assessing assay performance and ensuring reliable quantification of target analytes.

columns.name: inhibition_adjust
columns.description: A yes/no flag indicating whether inhibitory effects were factored into the PCR target concentration calculation and value. A “yes” value means that the measured PCR target concentration was adjusted for suppressed signals due to interfering substances. Incorporating inhibition adjustments prevents underestimation of target levels.

columns.name: ntc_amplify
columns.description: A yes/no flag indicating target amplification above the threshold for a positive detection in the no template control reaction(s). A no template control is a negative control reaction run without the addition of nucleic acid template that is used to detect potential contamination. A "yes" value indicates a positive NTC result which suggests contamination and the experiment is no longer valid.

columns.name: pcr_target
columns.description: The target of the PCR quantification (pathogen or antimicrobial resistance (AMR) groups)

columns.name: pcr_gene_target_agg
columns.description: The PCR gene used to quantify PCR target. Multiple gene targets may be tested, in which case they would be aggregated to reflect the various targets.

columns.name: pcr_target_avg_conc
columns.description: Concentration of the PCR target back-calculated to unconcentrated sample basis; value is "0" if no amplification occurred, using the definition of amplification described in 'ntc_amplify'; otherwise, the estimated concentration was entered; does not adjust for matrix recovery efficiency. Only the technical or biological replicate concentrations for the same pcr_gene_target are averaged.

columns.name: pcr_target_units
columns.description: Units of PCR target sample concentration

columns.name: lod_sewage
columns.description: PCR target limit of detection (LOD) is the lowest analyte concentration that can be reliably detected by a PCR assay. This value is the LOD concentration that has been back-calculated to the PCR target concentration PRIOR to sample concentration to adjust for any concentrations or dilutions performed on samples.

columns.name: pcr_target_avg_conc_lin
columns.description: Concentration of the PCR target on a per sample amount basis; this is merely 'pcr_target_avg_conc', where all values are on a linear (not log10) concentration basis. The PCR target is defined in the field, 'pcr_target' and its associated target gene in 'pcr_gene_target'.

columns.name: pcr_target_flowpop_lin
columns.description: Flow-population-normalized PCR target concentration on linear scale; calculated as concentration*flow/population. The PCR target is defined in the field, 'pcr_target' and its associated target gene in 'pcr_gene_target'.

columns.name: pcr_target_mic_lin
columns.description: Microbial-normalized PCR target concentration on linear scale; calculated as (concentration of pathogen) / (concentration of microbial endogenous control). The PCR target is defined in the field, 'pcr_target' and its associated target gene in 'pcr_gene_target'.

columns.name: hum_frac_target_mic
columns.description: Name of microbial target used to estimate human fecal content

columns.name: hum_frac_mic_conc
columns.description: Concentration of microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'

columns.name: hum_frac_mic_unit
columns.description: Concentration units of microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'

columns.name: rec_eff_percent
columns.description: Recovery efficiency percent calculates the recovery (or loss) of an exogenous organism ("rec_eff_target_name") from a wastewater sample after it has been fully processed (e.g., concentration and extraction) and measured by molecular detection methods such as PCR. Recovery efficiency percent is calculated by comparing the total measured gene copies in the fully processed sample (e.g., nucleic acid extract) to the measured or theoretical gene copy value of the initial spike-in concentration that was added to the wastewater sample prior to processing ("rec_eff_spike_conc"). “rec_eff_percent” = [((Measured "rec_eff_target_name" gene copies)/("rec_eff_spike_conc" gene copies)) x 100].

columns.name: rec_eff_target_name
columns.description: Name of the recovery efficiency control target that is spiked in to the sample process to assess recovery efficiency. The measured concentration of this variable is used to calculate "rec_eff_percent."

columns.name: rec_eff_spike_matrix
columns.description: Matrix into which the recovery efficiency control target is spiked to assess recovery efficiency:  "rec_eff_percent."

columns.name: rec_eff_spike_conc
columns.description: Spike concentration, on average, of the recovery control on a per sample volume basis. This value is used to assess the recovery efficiency: "rec_eff_percent".

columns.name: date_updated
columns.description: Timestamp of processing run used in posted data.  "As of " stamp.

attachments.filename: CDC Wastewater Public Datasets Data Dictionary v1.1.xlsx
attachments.name: CDC Wastewater Public Datasets Data Dictionary v1.1.xlsx

Data Quality.Footnotes: Wastewater concentrations at different sampling locations might be collected at different frequencies and obtained using different laboratory methods. It is not advised that wastewater concentrations be compared across sampling locations.
Data Quality.Suggested Citation: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Wastewater Surveillance System Analytics Data Set, year(s), as compiled from data provided through National Wastewater Surveillance System in the Rapid Response Research and Surveillance Branch (R3SB).

metadata.rowLabel: Wastewater sample data for measles per day of collection from a sampling site
